_comment: Operator-maintained register of Federal Acquisition Supply Chain Security Act (FASCSA) covered-article exclusion/removal orders. Authority: 41 U.S.C. §1323 (FASCSA of 2018); FAR Subpart 4.23; 41 CFR Part 201-1. Per cloud-evidence/CLAUDE.md REO Rule 4, FASC-order specifics are operator-supplied real data: the operator confirms the current FASC orders index (https://www.cisa.gov/fascsa) on the quarterly cadence documented in RUNBOOK.md and edits this PR-reviewed file. W.W1 ingests whatever entries are present; live PDF auto-extraction of the FASC orders index is deferred until core/pdf-table-extract.ts ships (LOOP-C.C3). Each entry's order_id is the operator-confirmed FASC identifier; where the operator has not yet confirmed an official identifier, a descriptive slug is used and confirmed=false flags it for review.
authority_citation: 41 U.S.C. §1323 (FASCSA of 2018); FAR Subpart 4.23; 41 CFR Part 201-1

orders.covered_article: Information and communications technology and services developed or provided by AO Kaspersky Lab, its predecessors, successors, parents, subsidiaries, or affiliates.
orders.issuing_authority: Federal Acquisition Security Council (FASC) per 41 U.S.C. §1323
orders.cross_reference: Harmonizes with NDAA FY2018 §1634 (Pub. L. 115-91) and DHS BOD 17-01.
orders.operator_note: Kaspersky covered-article exclusion. The FASC's exclusion harmonizes with the long-standing §1634 statutory prohibition and BOD 17-01. Operator must confirm the current official FASC order identifier against the CISA FASCSA index.
